title: DOCKER Validate Container Port Mapping in Compose File

statement.markdown: Given a Docker Compose file, write a function to validate the port mappings. The function should check if the port mappings are valid (i.e., the host port is not already in use by another service), and if the container port is within the valid range (0-65535).
statement.inputFormat: An object representing the Docker Compose file, with services and their corresponding port mappings.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the port mappings are valid.
statement.notes: The function should not modify the original Compose file.

constraints.description: Number of services in the Compose file
Number of port mappings per service

examples.explanation: The port mappings are valid for both services.
The port 8000 is already in use by the web service, so the port mappings are invalid.

hints.text: Check the port mappings for each service separately.
